evals.prompt: I have a Next.js 14 app. Run the full sustainability audit — SCI, WSG, accessibility, everything.
evals.expected_output: Runs 9-phase workflow: explore codebase, measure SCI, WSG compliance check, accessibility audit, performance review, testing, prioritization, baselines, CLAUDE.md update.
assertions: Starts by exploring the codebase structure and stack
Measures or attempts to measure SCI with a defined functional unit
Checks WSG guidelines (at least a subset of the 80)
Runs or configures Lighthouse for accessibility

evals.prompt: audit di sostenibilità per il mio progetto Python Flask — è un'API REST, niente frontend
evals.expected_output: Adapts the audit for a backend-only project: SCI per API call, skip WSG (no frontend), focus on server efficiency.
assertions: Recognizes this is a backend-only project
Defines functional unit as 'per API call' or similar

evals.prompt: Can you just do a quick sustainability check? I don't need the full 9-phase audit, just the highlights.
evals.expected_output: Suggests using /gc-check-sustainability instead, or provides a condensed version focusing on the highest-impact items.
assertions: Recognizes the user wants a lighter check
Either redirects to gc-check-sustainability or provides condensed version
Covers at minimum: green hosting, basic WSG, page weight
Does not run the full 9-phase workflow unnecessarily
